title: Spot-on-lawn (halo) assay for screening viruses in cultures and isolates
author: Kenneth M. Stedman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.eagbabw

steps.title: Gelrite plates are preincubated ca
steps.instruction: Gelrite plates are preincubated ca. 10 min at 80˚C to dry. 00:10:00

steps.title: 10 mL of Sulfolobus medium with ca
steps.instruction: 10 mL of Sulfolobus medium with ca. 0.2 % (w/v) Gelrite is boiled to dissolve the Gelrite.

steps.title: This “softlayer” is allowed to cool slightly (to ca
steps.instruction: This “softlayer” is allowed to cool slightly (to ca. 80˚C).

steps.title: Approximately 3 mL of softlayer are added to ca
steps.instruction: Approximately 3 mL of softlayer are added to ca. 0.2 mL of exponentially growing host cells, generally Sulfolobus solfataricus , and spread on a plate by swirling.

steps.title: After the Gelrite solidifies, 1–2 µL...
steps.instruction: After the Gelrite solidifies, 1–2 µL of culture or supernatant to be screened is spotted on the plate.

steps.title: For a positive control, 1 µL of a 0
steps.instruction: For a positive control, 1 µL of a 0.01% (v/v) Triton X-100 solution is spotted.

steps.title: Plates are incubated as above for...
steps.instruction: Plates are incubated as above for 2–3 d and plates examined for clearing around spots (Fig. 1D in guidelines). 72:00:00
